pom.xml.general_idea: Update the project's target Java version property from 17 to 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without this change, the project continues to compile and run against Java 17 semantics, failing to utilize Java 21 features or enforce Java 21 compatibility.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade jacoco-maven-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older JaCoCo versions may fail to instrument or report coverage for classes compiled with Java 21 due to class file version mismatches.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-checkstyle-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older Checkstyle versions may fail to parse or analyze Java 21 syntax or class files.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-compiler-plugin to a version that supports the 'release' flag and Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older compiler plugin versions may not support the 'release' configuration option or may fail to compile Java 21 source code.

pom.xml.general_idea: Upgrade maven-war-plugin to a version compatible with Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Older WAR plugin versions may encounter issues processing class files or dependencies generated for Java 21.

pom.xml.general_idea: Set the scope of jakarta.annotation-api to 'provided' to avoid packaging conflicts.
pom.xml.why_exists: In Java 21 environments with Jakarta EE, the annotation API is often provided by the runtime; including it in the artifact can cause classpath conflicts or OSGi issues.

pom.xml.general_idea: Replace deprecated 'source' and 'target' compiler options with the unified 'release' option.
pom.xml.why_exists: The 'source' and 'target' options are deprecated in favor of 'release' in modern Java toolchains; using 'release' ensures consistent API usage and avoids deprecation warnings or future removal.

pom.xml.general_idea: Add 'release' configuration to the test compiler plugin to ensure tests are compiled for Java 21.
pom.xml.why_exists: Without explicit 'release' configuration, the test compiler might default to an older Java version or inherit inconsistent settings, leading to runtime incompatibilities for tests.
